title: cyanus atratus
intro: Cyanus atratus, also known as the Cornflower, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, known for its vibrant blue flowers and edible leaves. The Cornflower has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Cornflower include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
